rulings.Case Title: ANDREW THOMPSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant NACCO Materials Handling Group, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to negligence and strict liability, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and Mr. Libby¿s deposition testimony create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. As to false representation and punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting those causes of action. Defendant¿s separate statement sis not demonstrate that defendant propounded contention discovery seeking all facts, witnesses and documents supporting those claims or that plaintiff¿s responses thereto were factually devoid. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GARY HOPSTER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Campbell Industries¿ motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the substantial factor issue and off calendar as to strict liability, false representation and punitive damages. As to substantial factor, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that plaintiff¿s exposure to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to an increased risk in developing his asbestos-related illness. Defendant¿s separate statement did not demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery seeking all facts, witnesses and documents regarding substantial factor causation or that plaintiff¿s responses thereto were factually devoid. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for strict liability and false representation and his request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GARY HOPSTER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Fraser¿s Boiler Service, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to strict liability and punitive damages and off calendar as to false representation. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Wilkerson¿s deposition testimony create a triable issue whether defendant¿s employees were in the vicinity of plaintiff, thereby precluding summary judgment in favor of defendant on ¿false identification,¿ the sole issue for which defendant sought summary judgment.. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration and Mr. Wilkerson¿s deposition testimony are overruled. As to strict liability, defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant was a manufacturer, seller, supplier or distributor of products or otherwise placed products into the stream of commerce. As to punitive damages, defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. Plaintiff dismissed his claim for false representation. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILEY UTTERBACK et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Brand Insulations, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILEY UTTERBACK et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Collins Electrical Company, Inc.S Notice Of Motion Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Collins Electrical Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Utterback was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Plaintiff¿s special interrogatory responses and deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant¿s separate statement did not demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Utterback. Even if defendant had satisfied its burden of production, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Utterback was exposed to asbestos-containing fireproofing disturbed by defendant and Dr. Richard Cohen¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant knew or should have known of the hazards of asbestos. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration and Dr. Cohen¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MAXIMILIANO RAZO et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and off calendar as to strict liability and punitive damages. As to negligence, Mr. Razo¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs dismissed their claim for strict liability and request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MAXIMILIANO RAZO et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Anchor Darling Valve Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to give proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. § 437c. The motion was e-filed on May 9, which is 73 days before July 21. = (220/HEK)
